title: Podman v3 can't pull any image because of default policy
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Podman show an error while trying pull any image from any public registry like docker.io or registry.fedoraproject.org

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
# podman pull fedora
Resolved short name "fedora" to a recorded short-name alias (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
  Running image docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest is rejected by policy.
Error: Source image rejected: Running image docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest is rejected by policy.
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.0.0-rc1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.19.2

  cpus: 4

  hostname: zen
  idMappings:

  memFree: 9947365376

  ociRuntime:

      crun version 0.17.5-86d6-dirty

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux

  security:

    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false

    selinuxEnabled: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""

  swapFree: 5578420224

  uptime: 74h 17m 34.53s (Approximately 3.08 days)

    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageStore:
    number: 34
  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
ii  containernetworking-plugins   0.9.0~3
ii  containers-common                1:1~4
ii  podman                                   3.0.0~0.4.rc1
ii  podman-plugins                       1.1.1~1
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body:
Also it's affect even layers copying between builds:
```
Error: error copying image "78b2869b282bf2f28a5e873d6ade079e83d77765223c7bcd180b77cbc8fe4751": Source image rejected: Running image containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on]@78b2869b282bf2f28a5e873d6ade079e83d77765223c7bcd180b77cbc8fe4751 is rejected by policy.
```

In podman v2 `/etc/containers/policy.json` just contain something like
```
    "default": [
        {
            "type": "insecureAcceptAnything"
        }
```
if I right remember, so I did it for v3 too as a hotfix

This looks like a packaging issue. @lsm5 PTAL.

I saw the same issue on IRC last week but I couldn't reproduce.

That file is shipped as part of containers-common,
 rpm -qVf /etc/containers/policy.json
Should be fixed with v3.0.0. Please reopen if issue persists.
